sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Downgrades OGE Energy to Underweight From Equalweight, Adjusts Price Target to $39 From $45
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has downgraded OGE Energy (OGE) from "Equalweight" to "Underweight" and reduced its price target from $45 to $39. This adjustment reflects a more cautious outlook on the utility company. The article also provides recent news and financial data for OGE Energy.

feed.title: Home Depot and Lowe’s shoppers beware – popular yard item recalled over safety fears
feed.summary: Approximately 6,100 Adirondack-style patio chairs sold at Lowe's and Home Depot under the brand names Adams RealComfort and StyleWell have been recalled by the United States Consumer Product Safety Commission. The recall is due to a manufacturing defect that causes the chairs to crack and potentially collapse, posing an injury risk. Owners are advised to stop using the chairs immediately and can return them to any Lowe's or Home Depot for a full refund.

feed.title: NetBrain Closes Majority Investment From Blackstone Growth
feed.summary: NetBrain, an AI NetOps platform, has closed a majority investment from Blackstone Growth to accelerate its global expansion and advance its AI-powered network automation. Concurrent with this investment, Bernadette Nixon has been appointed CEO, succeeding founder Lingping Gao who will become Chair of the Board. The investment aims to support innovation and meet the growing demand for intelligent, intent-based automation in network operations.

feed.title: Doug Bowser jumps ship from Nintendo for Hasbro
feed.summary: Doug Bowser, the recently retired President and Chief Operating Officer of Nintendo of America Inc., has been appointed to the Board of Directors at Hasbro, Inc. Bowser brings extensive leadership experience from his decade at Nintendo, where he oversaw the Nintendo Switch's success and the launch of the Nintendo Switch 2, and previous roles at Electronic Arts and Procter & Gamble. Hasbro's board highlighted his expertise in consumer brands and franchise management as invaluable for their growth strategy.

feed.title: Microchip Expands PolarFire Family of FPGAs
feed.summary: Microchip Technology has expanded its PolarFire FPGA smart embedded video ecosystem to support deterministic, low-power, and high-bandwidth video transport. The updated ecosystem now includes serial digital interface (SDI) and quad CoaXPress (CXP) IP cores, enabling complete sensor-to-output pipelines for various applications. This solution aims to reduce bill-of-materials, consolidate systems, and incorporate security features, while offering native sensor support for Sony SLVS-EC interfaces.

feed.title: NuScale Power, Constellation Energy, and Other Nuclear Stocks That Could Surge
feed.summary: The article highlights that nuclear power stocks, including NuScale Power and Constellation Energy, could experience significant gains. Technical signals suggest these stocks are emerging from a reset phase and are poised for their next upward movement. The piece focuses on the potential for growth in the nuclear energy sector.

feed.title: Kroger breaks ground on massive Crawfordsville store project
feed.summary: Kroger is investing $38 million to construct its largest-format Marketplace store to date in Crawfordsville, Ind. This new store, spanning two and a half football fields, will feature new offerings like Murray's Cheese, Starbucks, fresh sushi, and an apparel section. The project is part of a larger $200 million investment in the Central Division, with the new store expected to open within the next year.

feed.title: Citigroup Lowers Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) Price Target to $39.00
feed.summary: Citigroup has reduced its price target for Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) from $48.00 to $39.00, maintaining a "neutral" rating. This adjustment comes amidst varied analyst opinions, with the stock currently holding an average "Hold" rating and a consensus price target of $46.19 from MarketBeat.com data. The company recently missed earnings estimates for the latest quarter, reporting $0.35 EPS against an expected $0.46.

feed.title: Old Second Bancorp (OSBC) Declares $0.07 Quarterly Dividend with 1.36% Yield
feed.summary: Old Second Bancorp (OSBC) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.07 per share, maintaining consistency and signaling stable profitability. This declaration results in a forward yield of 1.36%, making it attractive to income-seeking investors. The dividend is payable on February 9, with record and ex-dividend dates on January 30, and the company projects future growth and a potential dividend boost following the Evergreen acquisition.

feed.title: AbbVie stock slips as traders watch Epkinly data and the next earnings clock
feed.summary: AbbVie shares experienced a slight dip of 0.4% in early afternoon trading, primarily driven by investor reaction to Epkinly (epcoritamab) Phase III data which showed progression-free survival but missed statistical significance for overall survival. Investors are now keenly focused on the company's full-year and fourth-quarter 2025 results, along with its 2026 outlook, scheduled for release on February 4. The market is closely watching how AbbVie's new oncology assets will contribute to growth as the company reduces its reliance on Humira cash flows amidst a competitive oncology landscape.

feed.title: Broadridge Financial Solutions stock hits 52-week low at $212.30
feed.summary: Broadridge Financial Solutions (BR) stock recently hit a 52-week low of $212.30, marking an 8.96% decline over the past year. This comes despite the company reporting strong fiscal Q1 2026 earnings, surpassing EPS and revenue forecasts, and acquiring Acolin to expand its global offerings. Analysts have issued mixed ratings, with DA Davidson maintaining a "Neutral" rating and Morgan Stanley adjusting its price target downwards while keeping an "Equalweight" rating.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Lowers Crown Castle (CCI) Price Target to $105 | C
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has lowered its price target for Crown Castle (CCI) to $105 while maintaining a 'Neutral' rating. This adjustment reflects a 10.26% decrease from the previous target of $117. The article also notes similar target reductions from other analysts and highlights Crown Castle's concentrated customer base and plans to divest its fiber business.

feed.title: Western Midstream Resets Delaware Basin Natural Gas Contracts in $610M Oxy Deal
feed.summary: Western Midstream Partners LP (WES) has signed a $610 million deal with its largest customer, Occidental Petroleum Corp. (Oxy), to replace existing cost-of-service natural gas contracts in the Delaware Basin with simpler fixed-fee agreements. These new contracts extend through the late 2030s and are part of WES's strategy to operate as a more mature, standalone midstream business. The agreement also involves Oxy reducing its ownership stake in WES and includes support for the growth of the North Loving II plant.

feed.title: Hesai Group's (NASDAQ:HSAI) Market Cap Touched US$4.5b Last Week, Benefiting Both Individual Investors Who Own 41% as Well as Institutions
feed.summary: Hesai Group's market capitalization reached US$4.5 billion last week after an 8.3% price gain, benefiting both individual investors (who own 41%) and institutions (40% stake). Individual investors collectively hold the largest share, granting them significant influence, while institutions also recognize the company's credibility. Insiders, notably CEO Kai Sun, own a substantial 17% of the company, indicating strong alignment with shareholder interests.

feed.title: Grocery chain 'definitely cheaper than Kroger' to open 4 new stores in 2026
feed.summary: Food Lion, a grocery chain with over 1,100 locations, plans to open four new stores in North and South Carolina in early 2026. This expansion reflects the company's commitment to providing affordable groceries and enhancing the shopping experience with features like walk-in garden coolers and fresh sushi. The new stores aim to strengthen Food Lion's position as a "trusted neighborhood grocer" in the region, adding to its existing footprint which shoppers online claim is "definitely" cheaper than rivals like Kroger.

feed.title: Wells Fargo becomes first major bank to relocate wealth operations headquarters to Florida
feed.summary: Wells Fargo is relocating its wealth management headquarters to West Palm Beach, Florida, making it the first major U.S. bank to base these operations in the state. Approximately 100 employees, primarily senior executives, will move to a new 50,000-square-foot office by the end of the year. This move highlights a growing trend of financial institutions moving from traditional hubs to business-friendly, lower-tax states.

feed.title: Wells Fargo's wealth management HQ said to move to West Palm Beach
feed.summary: Wells Fargo is reportedly moving its wealth management headquarters to West Palm Beach, following a broader trend of financial firms relocating to South Florida. The San Francisco-based company has signed a lease for the new location. This move signifies a significant shift for the major financial institution.

feed.title: Why Advanced Micro Devices Shares Are Climbing Today
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock rose on news that Meta Platforms is reportedly set to purchase AMD's MI455X AI accelerators. This move suggests Meta may reduce its in-house chip development in favor of AMD's hardware, reinforcing a strong partnership between the two companies. AMD, which saw Meta as its top AI GPU customer in 2025, is experiencing increased demand and significant stock performance, with shares up nearly 7% year-to-date and 90% over the last 12 months.

feed.title: Viking Revolution arrives at Walmart stores: what products can you find?
feed.summary: Viking Revolution, a men's grooming brand, is expanding its availability to 900 Walmart stores across the United States. This move allows customers to purchase core products like beard oil, balm, wash, conditioner, sea salt hairspray, shave cream, and solid cologne more easily. The brand, founded in 2016, focuses on practical, effective products for an authentic vision of masculinity and has grown significantly since its digital-native origins.

feed.title: TransDigm Announces $2.2B Acquisition of PMA Aftermarket Firms
feed.summary: TransDigm Group, a leading global producer, designer, and supplier of aerospace components, has announced a significant $2.2 billion acquisition of two privately held companies that manufacture proprietary aftermarket components. This strategic move is expected to expand TransDigm's portfolio in the aftermarket sector, subject to U.S. regulatory approvals. The acquisition aims to strengthen TransDigm's position within the aerospace maintenance and modifications market.

feed.title: Türkiye's state oil company in talks with Chevron for exploration partnership
feed.summary: Türkiye's state energy company, Turkish Petroleum Corp. (TPAO), is in negotiations with Chevron Corp. for a joint oil and gas exploration partnership, according to a Turkish official. This potential collaboration aligns with Ankara's strategy to boost domestic energy production and marks an improvement in U.S.-Turkish relations, following a recent agreement between TPAO and Exxon Mobil Corp. TPAO aims to reduce Türkiye's reliance on imported energy and has been expanding its international operations and fleet of specialized vessels.

feed.title: Russell Investments, ICI Join BlackRock, Charles Schwab Among Those Announcing Trump Account Contribution Plans
feed.summary: Several corporations, including Russell Investments, BlackRock, and Charles Schwab, have announced plans to match contributions to the new "Trump Accounts," a children's savings program launching in July 2025. This program, authorized by the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, provides a $1,000 U.S. Treasury contribution to tax-advantaged accounts for eligible children born between 2025 and 2028. While some employers are committing, many are still awaiting further guidance from the IRS and other regulators regarding the implementation and administration of these accounts.

feed.title: Waters faces shareholder suits as $17.5B BD merger approaches completion
feed.summary: Waters Corp. is facing shareholder lawsuits regarding its impending $17.5 billion merger with BD. The lawsuits allege that the proxy statement distributed to shareholders was missing crucial details about the deal. The merger is nearing completion despite these legal challenges.

feed.title: Teledyne’s Detectors Successfully Launch Aboard NASA’s BlackCAT CubeSat Mission
feed.summary: Teledyne Technologies announced that its Space Imaging division's Speedster HyViSI Focal Plane Arrays successfully launched aboard NASA's BlackCAT CubeSat Mission on January 11, 2026. This mission, led by Pennsylvania State University, uses Teledyne's detectors to study x-ray transient events and the early universe. The advanced detectors offer high sensitivity to soft x-ray photons, low power consumption, and radiation-hard performance, making them ideal for space-based x-ray astronomy.

feed.title: Head of Wells Fargo Advisors on channel choice; a proven RIA organic growth engine; Raymond James’ big moves
feed.summary: This article covers significant developments in the financial planning industry, including Raymond James' acquisition of Clark Capital Management Group and recruitment from Merrill, changes in charitable giving rules impacting high-net-worth clients, and the importance of marketing for RIA organic growth. It also features insights from Sol Gindi, head of Wells Fargo Advisors, on advisor independence and the firm's multichannel model.

feed.title: Behind the Scenes of IBM's Latest Options Trends
feed.summary: Whales have shown a noticeably bearish stance on IBM (NYSE: IBM) based on recent options trading activity, with 44% of investors opening bearish trades compared to 33% bullish. Significant investors anticipate IBM's price to range between $190.0 and $380.0 in the next three months. Despite this, IBM's stock is currently down -3.12% at $296.13, and analysts maintain an average price target of $343.75, with some upgrading their ratings.

feed.title: Can Prologis Keep the Winning Streak Alive in Q4 Despite Challenges?
feed.summary: Prologis (PLD) is set to announce its Q4 2025 earnings, with analysts anticipating a rise in rental revenues and healthy leasing activity, though high interest expenses remain a concern. Despite a projected 4% decrease in Q4 FFO per share, the company has consistently beaten Zacks Consensus Estimates in prior quarters. The U.S. industrial real estate market showed resilience in Q4 2025 with increased net absorption, a steady vacancy rate, and a growing construction pipeline, factors that could support Prologis's performance.

feed.title: 3M shares fall on subdued 2026 outlook
feed.summary: 3M Co.'s 2026 profit outlook fell slightly short of Wall Street expectations, causing shares to drop. CEO Bill Brown is working to revamp operations, revive product pipelines, and increase efficiencies, with efforts in 2025 topping expectations. The company is also exploring divesting low-growth assets and focusing on high-growth sectors like data centers and nuclear facilities, despite a modest outlook for 2026.

feed.title: Herc Holdings Inc (HRI) Shares Down 3.97% on Jan 20
feed.summary: Herc Holdings Inc (HRI) shares fell 3.97% in mid-day trading on January 20th, reaching an intraday low of $150.94 before closing at $151.68. This places HRI 28.52% below its 52-week high and 57.70% above its 52-week low. Wall Street analysts forecast an average target price of $175.50, implying a 15.70% upside, and an "Outperform" recommendation.

feed.title: Lululemon Pauses Online Sales of New Collection After Complaints
feed.summary: Lululemon Athletica Inc. temporarily removed its new "Get Low" collection of workout apparel from its website just days after its debut, following consumer complaints. The company stated that it paused online sales in North America to better understand guest feedback and provide product education, though the collection remains available in physical stores.

feed.title: McKinney Kroger Store to Close Doors, Another to Open
feed.summary: Kroger announced the closure of a store in midtown McKinney, Texas, in March as part of a company-wide efficiency initiative. Despite the closure, Kroger plans to open a new Marketplace store in west McKinney, anchoring the Custer Frontier Marketplace. Displaced employees will be offered positions at other DFW-area Kroger stores.

feed.title: Fair Isaac Corp: Can FICO’s AI Credit Engine Justify Its Soaring Stock Price?
feed.summary: Fair Isaac Corp (FICO) stock has seen a sharp climb, driven by its AI-powered credit engine and strong performance in the software sector, trading near record highs. While Wall Street is largely bullish, with analysts reiterating positive ratings and investors betting on its AI-driven decisioning software, concerns linger about whether its fundamentals fully support its rich valuation. The company's strategic focus on its cloud-based FICO Platform and expansion into new verticals aims to sustain its growth trajectory amidst increasing regulatory scrutiny.

feed.title: Syngene International extends long-term research collaboration with Bristol Myers Squibb until 2035
feed.summary: Syngene International has extended its research collaboration with Bristol Myers Squibb until 2035, building on a partnership that began in 1998. This expanded agreement will broaden Syngene's integrated services across the entire drug development lifecycle, from discovery to manufacturing and clinical trials. The collaboration aims to accelerate the delivery of transformative medicines and includes plans for new capabilities and infrastructure to advance innovative therapies.

feed.title: The Goldman Sachs Group Boosts Huntington Ingalls Industries (NYSE:HII) Price Target to $425.00
feed.summary: The Goldman Sachs Group has increased its price target for Huntington Ingalls Industries (NYSE:HII) from $384.00 to $425.00, reiterating a "buy" rating for the aerospace company. Other analysts have also adjusted their ratings and price targets, with the stock currently holding an average "Hold" rating and a target price of $343.63. The article also details recent stock performance, quarterly earnings, insider trading activity, and institutional investor changes.

feed.title: Everest hands Cellura and Gallagher new senior reinsurance roles
feed.summary: Everest Group has announced two key leadership appointments within its facultative and hybrid reinsurance business. Michael Cellura has been appointed as the head of the unit, while Trevor Gallagher will serve as its chief operating officer.

feed.title: Ford pushes affordable EVs despite major strategy shift
feed.summary: Ford is adjusting its U.S. business strategy by increasing its focus on gasoline and hybrid vehicles due to a slowdown in EV demand, though it still plans to release affordable electric vehicles. The company is facing significant financial restructuring costs and challenges from changing political policies on emissions and trade, which complicate long-term planning. Despite these hurdles, Ford aims to improve quality, execution, and maintain market share, while Executive Chair Bill Ford emphasized the importance of adaptability in a fluid economic and political landscape.

feed.title: AI Needs Power Now-Bloom Energy and American Electric Power Deliver
feed.summary: The energy sector is seeing a shift from green dreams to commercial necessity, driven by the massive energy demands of artificial intelligence. Bloom Energy and American Electric Power have signed a landmark $2.65 billion deal for 1 gigawatt of solid oxide fuel cells to power data centers, bypassing traditional grid limitations. This collaboration addresses the immediate need for reliable baseload power for AI infrastructure, positioning both companies as key players in fulfilling current energy demands.

feed.title: Analysts’ Top Financial Picks: Prudential Financial (PRU), First Horizon (FHN)
feed.summary: Analysts have issued neutral ratings for Prudential Financial (PRU) and First Horizon (FHN), indicating market experts are neither strongly bullish nor bearish on these stocks. Morgan Stanley analyst Bob Huang maintained a Hold rating on Prudential Financial with a price target of $120.00, while Evercore ISI's John Pancari also maintained a Hold rating on First Horizon with a price target of $26.00. The consensus among analysts for Prudential Financial is a Hold with a 4.1% upside, and for First Horizon, it is a Moderate Buy with a 10.5% upside, despite a recent downgrade to Hold by TipRanks – OpenAI.

feed.title: Oh, Baby! KRISPY KREME® Celebrates First Birthday of Baby Delivered in Krispy Kreme Parking Lot with Zero Delivery this Wednesday-Thursday
feed.summary: Krispy Kreme is celebrating the first birthday of "Baby Dallas," who was born in a Krispy Kreme parking lot during a snowstorm on January 22, 2025. To mark the occasion, the company is offering zero delivery fees on all doughnut orders placed through its website and app this Wednesday and Thursday. The promotion also highlights Krispy Kreme's new Winter Seasonal Collection, featuring unique doughnut flavors.

feed.title: Fifth Third CEO reveals early closing date for $10.9 billion Comerica acquisition
feed.summary: Fifth Third Bancorp is accelerating its $10.9 billion acquisition of Comerica Inc., with CEO Tim Spence announcing an early closing date of February 1st. The bank plans an aggressive expansion in Texas following the acquisition, including opening 150 new branches and launching a significant consumer marketing campaign, which will be the first for those locations in over a decade. This move underscores Fifth Third's strategy to strengthen its market presence and integrate Comerica's operations efficiently.

feed.title: Globant S.A. (GLOB) PT Raised to $75 at UBS
feed.summary: UBS analyst Leonardo Olmos has raised the price target for Globant S.A. (NYSE: GLOB) to $75.00 from $70.00. The analyst maintained a "neutral" rating for the company. This article was published on January 20, 2026, at 5:48 AM EST.

feed.title: Deadline Alert: F5, Inc. (FFIV) Shareholders Who Lost Money
feed.summary: Glancy Prongay & Murray LLP is reminding F5, Inc. (FFIV) investors who suffered losses during the Class Period (October 28, 2024, to October 27, 2025) of the upcoming February 17, 2026 deadline to file a lead plaintiff motion in a securities fraud lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that F5 concealed a significant security incident involving a nation-state threat actor gaining unauthorized access to its systems, which led to two significant drops in F5's stock price after the disclosures. Investors are urged to contact Glancy Prongay & Murray LLP to inquire about pursuing claims.

feed.title: Analysts’ Top Financial Picks: Prudential Financial (PRU), First Horizon (FHN)
feed.summary: Analysts have issued neutral ratings for Prudential Financial (PRU) and First Horizon (FHN). Morgan Stanley maintained a Hold rating for Prudential Financial with a $120 price target, while Evercore ISI maintained a Hold rating for First Horizon with a $26 price target. The consensus for Prudential Financial is Hold, and for First Horizon, it is a Moderate Buy.

feed.title: Market Chatter: Chevron, Turkey to Collaborate on Oil and Gas Exploration
feed.summary: Chevron and Turkey are collaborating on oil and gas exploration, as indicated by recent market chatter. This news was published amidst other market updates, including Unity Software's fourth-quarter financial results which showed a significant revenue increase but also a quarterly loss. The full article content discussing the collaboration is behind a login wall, suggesting further details are available to members.

feed.title: Has Alphabet Stock Hit Its Top at $4 Trillion?
feed.summary: Alphabet (GOOGL) recently reached a $4 trillion market cap, following Nvidia (NVDA) and signing a partnership with Apple (AAPL) for AI technology. However, GOOGL experienced losses after hitting this peak, a pattern observed with other megacaps like Apple and Nvidia, suggesting a "hangover" effect. While short-term pullbacks are common after reaching such significant market cap milestones, historical data indicates a positive long-term outlook for these stocks, with most beating the S&P 500 over a year.

feed.title: WIZARDS OF THE COAST PARTNERS WITH TOP CREATORS TO BRING DUNGEONS & DRAGONS TO FORTNITE
feed.summary: Wizards of the Coast, a subsidiary of Hasbro, has announced a collaboration with Chartis and Teravision Games, leading Fortnite Island creators, to bring Dungeons & Dragons-inspired experiences to Fortnite. This initiative includes five custom islands built by D&D fans using Epic's UEFN toolset, offering new ways for players to engage with the D&D universe within Fortnite, featuring familiar locations and legendary creatures from the Forgotten Realms. Players can now explore these new experiences using specific Island Codes, marking an experimental step to introduce D&D to a broader audience.

feed.title: Roper Technologies stock hits 52-week low at $407.01
feed.summary: Roper Technologies (ROP) stock has hit a 52-week low of $407.01, representing a 23.2% decline over the past year. Despite its "GOOD" financial health rating and 35 consecutive years of dividend payments, the company faces market volatility, AI threats, and cyclical headwinds, leading to recent analyst downgrades. Roper is addressing these challenges by appointing new AI executives.

feed.title: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Nvidia, Netflix, Intel, Sandisk, 3M, Coinbase, Newmont, RAPT, Fastenal, and More
feed.summary: Stocks, including major tech companies like Nvidia, Netflix, and Intel, experienced a downturn on Tuesday. The market's decline was attributed to investor concerns over President Donald Trump’s proposal to impose tariffs on eight NATO countries. These tariffs are reportedly a tactic to pressure the countries into allowing the U.S. administration to acquire Greenland.

feed.title: Globalstar, Cogent, Hewlett Packard Enterprise, Viasat, and Getty Images Stocks Trade Up and Down, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Several stocks, including Globalstar, Cogent, Hewlett Packard Enterprise, Viasat, and Getty Images, experienced varied trading in the afternoon session due to escalating geopolitical tensions between the US and the EU. This uncertainty led to a "risk-off" market sentiment and a jump in the VIX. Viasat's shares, in particular, showed significant volatility, though recent positive news like a raised price target from Morgan Stanley and new business developments have contributed to its year-to-date gain.

feed.title: These Analysts Revise Their Forecasts On Regions Financial Following Q4 Earnings
feed.summary: Regions Financial Corp (NYSE: RF) reported weaker-than-expected Q4 earnings, missing analyst estimates for both earnings per share and sales. Following the announcement, analysts from Piper Sandler and Wells Fargo revised their price targets, with Piper Sandler raising its target to $31 and Wells Fargo lowering its target to $28. Regions Financial's CEO highlighted solid growth in 2025 and an optimistic outlook for 2026, citing improving economic trends and strong capital position.
